rolling_30d.summary: Acima's local pages site experienced significant seasonal headwinds in the last 30 days, with sessions declining 40.8% MoM—a typical post-promotional pullback after strong February momentum. However, engagement quality improved notably: engaged sessions grew 11.7% MoM and engagement rate jumped 9.3 pp despite lower overall traffic, signaling stronger visitor intent. YoY, the picture is dramatically positive: sessions are up 243.2%, driven almost entirely by a 3061.4% surge in Direct traffic (now 69.2% of mix), alongside stable organic search (25.3%, +8.5% MoM). High-value conversion events show mixed signals—apply_now declined 3.6% MoM and 20.7% YoY, while calls dropped 16.6% MoM and 38.0% YoY, but location-intent events (store_locate +12.5% MoM, locate_me +11.5% MoM) and outbound_clicks (+30.5% YoY) remain robust. YTD 2026 traffic stands at 193,679 sessions, a compelling +374.3% vs. YTD 2025, with Q1 averaging ~65k sessions/month—suggesting sustained growth momentum despite this month's seasonal dip.

quick_wins.title: Homepage dominance & engagement surge
quick_wins.detail: The / homepage drove 3,767 sessions with 70.2% engagement rate (+98.5% MoM), capturing strong organic search and direct traffic. This is the clearest win—double the engagement of any other page.

quick_wins.title: Location-finder intent acceleration
quick_wins.detail: store_locate events grew 12.5% MoM and 67.0% YoY; locate_me surged 11.5% MoM and 110.3% YoY. These high-intent events suggest strong mobile/local search behavior and are primed for conversion optimization.

quick_wins.title: Organic Search stability & efficiency
quick_wins.detail: Organic Search held steady at 25.3% of traffic with +8.5% MoM growth, delivering 11,704 sessions to a shrinking overall pie—indicating better keyword performance and SERP positioning.

potential_warnings.title: Calls conversion collapse
potential_warnings.detail: Phone call initiations plummeted 16.6% MoM and 38.0% YoY to just 326 events. This high-value conversion signal is in sharp decline and warrants review of call-to-action clarity, CTR placement, and mobile UX.

potential_warnings.title: Apply-now application decline
potential_warnings.detail: apply_now events fell 3.6% MoM and 20.7% YoY (1,239 events), the primary conversion funnel metric. Combined with falling calls, this suggests friction in the application journey or reduced qualified visitor intent.

potential_warnings.title: Directional traffic anomaly & sustainability risk
potential_warnings.detail: Direct traffic surged 3061.4% YoY but fell 51.5% MoM, now comprising 69.2% of sessions. While YoY growth is extraordinary, the sharp MoM decline and heavy reliance on Direct (vs. organic) raises questions about campaign seasonality, brand lift sustainability, and organic SEO dependency.

potential_warnings.title: Location-to-conversion funnel leakage
potential_warnings.detail: Location-finder events (store_locate + locate_me) grew strongly (+12.5% and +11.5% MoM) but calls and apply_now both declined, suggesting high-intent users are not converting after finding a store. Post-localization experience and CTA clarity need audit.

biggest_opportunities.title: Resurrect and optimize the calls funnel
biggest_opportunities.detail: Calls are down 38% YoY and 16.6% MoM. With location-finder events strong, users are finding stores but not calling. Test click-to-call UX, remove friction, A/B call-button placement, and ensure mobile numbers are prominent on location landing pages.

biggest_opportunities.title: Capitalize on location-intent momentum with post-localization CTAs
biggest_opportunities.detail: store_locate and locate_me events are +67% and +110% YoY respectively. Double down by adding urgency/offer CTAs immediately after a user locates a store, and track downstream conversion (calls, apply_now) by location page.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand organic search visibility & capacity
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Search is the only channel showing YoY stability (+0.4% flat) and healthy MoM growth (+8.5%), delivering steady qualified traffic. Allocate budget to SEO content expansion (state/region pages similar to /puerto-rico, /new-jersey, /california) to capture long-tail local intent.

biggest_opportunities.title: Audit and resolve homepage-to-application funnel drop-off
biggest_opportunities.detail: The homepage (/) is the #1 traffic engine (3,767 sessions, 70.2% engagement) but apply_now is down 20.7% YoY. Add secondary CTAs, streamline navigation to apply, and measure homepage-to-apply conversion rate to identify leakage.

last_calendar_month.summary: March 2026 delivered strong YoY growth (+267.6% sessions) but faced notable MoM contraction (-29.9%), signaling seasonal softness after two exceptionally strong months (Jan +403.3%, Feb +457.0%). Engagement rate improved 7.9 pp MoM to 18.2%, though it remains 33.4 pp below March 2025, indicating quality gains amid volume decline. Direct traffic dominated at 71.2% of sessions but dropped 41.0% MoM, while organic search stabilized with +21.7% MoM and +0.9% YoY, suggesting paid/branded campaigns peaked in February. High-intent events showed mixed signals: store_locate and locate_me surged (+25.0% and +27.7% MoM respectively, both +60%+ YoY), but calls declined 9.2% MoM and 35.8% YoY—a concerning trend for a financing business. Apply_now events held relatively steady (+8.8% MoM) despite overall traffic decline, while outbound_clicks remained strong at 12,226 events (+4.0% MoM, +29.6% YoY). Q1 2026 closed with 193,679 YTD sessions, a +374.3% jump versus Q1 2025, validating the overall trajectory despite March's pullback.

quick_wins.title: Organic Search Momentum
quick_wins.detail: Organic search grew +21.7% MoM to 12,157 sessions (23.8% of traffic) while maintaining stable YoY performance (+0.9%). This channel is becoming a reliable traffic engine as paid spend normalizes.

quick_wins.title: Location-Intent Surge
quick_wins.detail: store_locate and locate_me events combined for 1,082 events in March, up +25.0% and +27.7% MoM respectively, with YoY growth exceeding +60%. This signals strong mobile/local intent and readiness for conversion.

quick_wins.title: Homepage Dominance & Engagement
quick_wins.detail: / landed 3,587 sessions with 70.1% engagement rate (+101.3% MoM, +1340.6% YoY), driving 2,516 engaged sessions. This page is clearly resonating and should be optimized further.

quick_wins.title: Apply_Now Resilience
quick_wins.detail: apply_now held at 1,297 events (+8.8% MoM) despite 29.9% overall traffic decline, demonstrating sticky conversion intent among remaining visitors.

potential_warnings.title: Call Volume Decline
potential_warnings.detail: Calls dropped 9.2% MoM and 35.8% YoY to 336 events, the steepest YoY decline among high-value conversions. For a financing product, phone conversions are critical; this reversal warrants investigation into messaging, CTAs, and phone routing.

potential_warnings.title: Month-over-Month Traffic Cliff
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions fell 29.9% from February's 72,904 to March's 51,095, with direct traffic plummeting 41.0% MoM. While YoY growth remains strong, the sharp MoM deceleration suggests campaign fatigue or budget exhaustion post-February peak.

potential_warnings.title: Get_Directions & Location Conversion Leakage
potential_warnings.detail: get_directions events collapsed 13.6% MoM and 53.9% YoY to just 70 events, despite store_locate strength. This disconnect suggests location finders aren't converting to navigation/visit intent as effectively.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement Rate YoY Gap
potential_warnings.detail: Engagement rate sits at 18.2% in March 2026 vs. 51.6% implied YoY (33.4 pp higher), indicating quality or audience composition has shifted. While MoM engagement improved, the YoY degradation deserves deeper cohort analysis.

biggest_opportunities.title: Revive Phone Conversion Channel
biggest_opportunities.detail: With calls down 35.8% YoY despite location intent growing, implement A/B testing on call button placement, prominence, and messaging—especially on high-engagement pages like / (70.1% engaged) and location pages. Consider click-to-call optimization and lead scoring to prioritize phone-ready traffic.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale Organic Search
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search is the only major channel showing stability and growth (+21.7% MoM, +0.9% YoY) without decay. Increase SEO investment in high-intent keywords around financing and location-based searches to offset direct traffic volatility and build sustainable growth.

biggest_opportunities.title: Optimize Location-to-Conversion Funnel
biggest_opportunities.detail: store_locate and locate_me are surging (+25%+ MoM), but get_directions collapsed (-53.9% YoY). Redesign post-location-finder experience with embedded directions, hours, phone numbers, and direct apply_now CTAs to capture high-intent users before they leave.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate Direct Traffic Collapse & Rebuild Brand Stability
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct dropped 41.0% MoM to 36,396 sessions despite a stunning +3443.9% YoY figure, indicating unsustainable peaks in early 2026. Audit January–February campaigns to identify which drove direct lift, then build sustainable brand/email nurture strategies to stabilize this channel.

biggest_opportunities.title: Test Regional Expansion on High-Engagement Pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: Pages like /maine/lewiston (+200% YoY engagement) and /puerto-rico (+25.0% MoM) show strong regional traction. Allocate budget to expand location-based landing pages and organic search for similar underperforming markets to replicate success.
